title: picea engelmannii var. typica
intro: Picea engelmannii var. typica, also known as Engelmann spruce, is a variety of coniferous tree native to the western United States and Canada. The resinous sap of the Engelmann spruce has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of the plant are believed to provide relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as alleviate symptoms of sore throats and coughs. The herbal preparations of Picea engelmannii var. typica include essential oils extracted from the resin, which can be used in topical applications, such as ointments and salves, to promote healing and soothe irritated skin.
